GLCT.module.title: GLUniverse — Clocks & Tracker
GLCT.hud.dragHint: Drag to reposition · double-click to switch standard / compact
GLCT.hud.gmTimeHint: GM only — exact time & stretch progress within this watch
GLCT.controls.rewindHint: Left-click to advance · Right-click to rewind
GLCT.controls.missionToggle: Set a mission — count down the stretches to a target time
GLCT.mission.title: Mission Countdown
GLCT.mission.hint: Pin a target and the stretch meter counts down to it: every stretch between now and the target stays individually visible so players can see exactly how many are left.
GLCT.settings.calendar.name: Active Calendar Definition
GLCT.settings.calendar.hint: The stored custom calendar used by this world.
GLCT.settings.events.name: Calendar Events & Holidays
GLCT.settings.events.hint: Stored events and holidays for the active calendar.
GLCT.settings.hudPosition.name: HUD Position
GLCT.settings.hudPosition.hint: Saved screen position of the HUD.
GLCT.settings.hudCollapsed.name: HUD Collapsed
GLCT.settings.sceneLight.name: Tint Scene With Shift
GLCT.settings.sceneLight.hint: Opt-in per scene: tint the canvas to match the current watch.
GLCT.settings.shiftMode.name: Track Time at Shift Level
GLCT.settings.shiftMode.hint: Focus the HUD on the current watch and hide the clock & stretch meter. Time still advances in stretches; this only changes the display. The GM can also flip this from the HUD dock.
GLCT.settings.stepMode.name: Default Step Snapping
GLCT.settings.stepMode.hint: When advancing, snap to the nearest stretch or shift boundary.
GLCT.editor.title: Calendar Editor
GLCT.shiftNames.title: Watch Names
GLCT.shiftNames.label: Edit Watch Names
GLCT.shiftNames.hint: Rename the four daily watches (shifts) shown in the HUD, in chronological order from midnight. Leave a field blank to use its default name.
GLCT.events.title: Events & Holidays
GLCT.events.name: Name
GLCT.events.notePrivateHint: Only the GM can see this
GLCT.calendarView.title: Calendar
GLCT.tracker.title: Trackers
GLCT.tracker.pickType: What kind of tracker would you like to add?
GLCT.tracker.emptyHint: No trackers yet — press + to add one.
GLCT.tracker.noGM: No GM is connected to roll this pool.
GLCT.tracker.field.name: Name
GLCT.tracker.field.subtitle: Subtitle
GLCT.tracker.field.label: Label (optional)
GLCT.weather.chipHint: Weather — click to open the Hex Flower
GLCT.weather.rollHint: Left-click to advance one step · Right-click to rewind
GLCT.weather.resetHint: Reset the walk to the start hex
GLCT.weather.resetConfirm: Reset the weather walk to its start hex? History is cleared.
GLCT.weather.notConfigured: No weather flower is configured yet.
GLCT.weather.field.name: Label
GLCT.weather.settings.enabled.name: Enable Weather
GLCT.weather.settings.enabled.hint: Opt in to the Hex Flower weather system. Ships off so calendar-only worlds are unaffected. A weather chip appears on the HUD once enabled and a flower is configured.
GLCT.weather.settings.cadenceMode.name: Weather Cadence
GLCT.weather.settings.cadenceMode.hint: Auto: the weather walks forward as in-game time passes. Manual: only the GM's Roll-now control advances it.
GLCT.weather.settings.cadencePeriod.name: Weather Step Period
GLCT.weather.settings.cadencePeriod.hint: How much in-game time passes per auto weather step.
GLCT.weather.settings.playerFlower.name: Reveal Flower to Players
GLCT.weather.settings.playerFlower.hint: When on, players can see the full Hex Flower (read-only). Otherwise they see only the current condition and recent history.
GLCT.weather.settings.showDice.name: Roll 3D Dice for Weather
GLCT.weather.settings.showDice.hint: When on, the Navigation Hex roll is thrown as real 3D dice (requires the Dice So Nice! module) on manual steps and single-period auto advances. Multi-day skips stay silent to avoid an animation flood.
GLCT.weather.editor.title: Weather Editor
GLCT.weather.editor.menuHint: Design the Hex Flower: per-season weather, the Navigation Hex, effects, presets, and JSON import/export.
GLCT.weather.editor.clickHex: Click a hex to edit it.
GLCT.weather.editor.imported: Weather loaded into the editor. Review, then Save.
GLCT.weather.editor.invalidIndex: Season “{season}” has invalid or duplicate hex indices.
GLCT.weather.editor.invalidDir: Season “{season}” has an invalid direction in its Navigation Hex.
GLCT.weather.editor.invalidDisallow: Season “{season}” has an invalid blocked direction on a hex.
GLCT.weather.editor.seasonalHint: When on, this region uses a different flower + Navigation Hex per calendar season. When off, one flower is used all year.
GLCT.weather.editor.seasonalOnHint: Seasonal: the calendar season picks the flower (edit each via the tabs below).
GLCT.weather.editor.seasonalOffHint: Single flower for all time — the calendar season is ignored; only the first flower is used.
GLCT.weather.editor.disallowHint: Checked directions are blocked from this hex: rolling one keeps the walk here. Each blocked face is marked with a red ✕ on the flower (the cookbook's red Ø).
GLCT.weather.region.label: Active region
GLCT.weather.region.switchHint: Switch which region drives the HUD and chat. Every region keeps its own weather.
GLCT.weather.region.deleteConfirm: Delete the region “{name}” and its weather walk? This can't be undone.
